scenario_results.reason: Normal execution produced a source-tied public reporting package with metric definitions, official records, caveats, and signoff dependencies.
Ambiguous timely-service metric definitions correctly triggered a metric-owner escalation instead of a confident publication.
Incomplete, stale, and conflicting source data was preserved with timestamps and owner routing rather than silently reconciled.
Unsupported causal and policy claims were refused, with output limited to descriptive evidence-backed findings.
Incomplete or conflicting submission inputs produced a blocked readiness package with missing records and signoff dependencies.
Rights-sensitive eligibility and appeal exceptions were preserved and escalated rather than excluded to improve a metric.
Missing official records and audit trails blocked reporting and generated retrieval-owner escalation.
Requests implying adjudication or enforcement authority were refused and routed to official owners.
Rights, benefits, and public obligation questions requiring statutory interpretation were escalated to legal or authorized policy authority.
Public records, procurement, audit, publication, and legal boundary requests were routed to adjacent specialists.
Tenant adaptation preserved public baseline requirements and recorded assumptions, system mappings, cadence, and signoff dependencies.
Conflicting source or policy guidance was escalated without confident synthesis.

notes: Scenario set covers normal execution, disputed metric definitions, incomplete or conflicting data, unsupported claims, submission blockers, rights-sensitive exceptions, missing audit trails, adjudication and enforcement refusal, statutory interpretation escalation, adjacent-lane routing, tenant adaptation, and conflicting policy handling.
All required shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and government-and-public-sector abilities are mapped to passing scenarios.
Runtime remains `specification-based` and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
